children.heading: Management Plan
children.label: 1 Management Plan
children.text: The term “Management Plan” means the management plan for the Recreation Area prepared under subsection (e)(2)(A).

children.heading: Map
children.label: 2 Map
children.text: The term “Map” means the map entitled “Northern Utah Lands Management Act-Overview” and dated .February 4, 2019

children.heading: Recreation Area
children.label: 3 Recreation Area
children.text: The term “Recreation Area” means the Ashley Karst National Recreation and Geologic Area established by subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 4 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Agriculture.

children.heading: State
children.label: 5 State
children.text: The term “State” means the State of Utah.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to valid existing rights, there is established the Ashley Karst National Recreation and Geologic Area in the State.

children.heading: Area included
children.label: 2 Area included
children.text: The Recreation Area shall consist of approximately 173,475 acres of land in the Ashley National Forest, as generally depicted on the Map.

children.heading: Purposes
children.label: c Purposes
children.text: The purposes of the Recreation Area are to conserve and protect the watershed, geological, recreational, wildlife, scenic, natural, cultural, and historic resources of the Recreation Area.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: As soon as practicable after , the Secretary shall prepare and submit to the Committee on Natural Resources and the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate a map and legal description of the Recreation Area.March 12, 2019

children.heading: Effect
children.label: 2 Effect
children.text: The map and legal description prepared under paragraph (1) shall have the same force and effect as if included in this section, except that the Secretary may correct minor errors in the map or legal description.

children.heading: Availability
children.label: 3 Availability
children.text: A copy of the map and legal description prepared under paragraph (1) shall be on file and available for public inspection in the appropriate offices of the Forest Service.

children.label: A
children.text: the laws generally applicable to the National Forest System, including the Forest and Rangeland Renewable Resources Planning Act of 1974 ( et seq.);16 U.S.C. 1600

children.label: B
children.text: this section; and

children.label: C
children.text: any other applicable law.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Secretary shall prepare a management plan for the Recreation Area.March 12, 2019

children.label: i
children.text: prepare the management plan in consultation and coordination with Uintah County, Utah, and affected Indian Tribes; and

children.label: ii
children.text: provide for public input in the preparation of the management plan.
further the purposes for which the Recreation Area is established; and
promote the long-term protection and management of the watershed and underground karst system of the Recreation Area.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as needed for emergency response or administrative purposes, the use of motorized vehicles in the Recreation Area shall be permitted only on roads and motorized routes designated in the Management Plan for the use of motorized vehicles.

children.heading: New roads
children.label: 2 New roads
children.text: No new permanent or temporary roads or other motorized vehicle routes shall be constructed within the Recreation Area after .March 12, 2019

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Necessary maintenance or repairs to existing roads designated in the Management Plan for the use of motorized vehicles, including necessary repairs to keep existing roads free of debris or other safety hazards, shall be permitted after , consistent with the requirements of this section.March 12, 2019

children.heading: Rerouting
children.label: B Rerouting
children.text: Nothing in this subsection prevents the Secretary from rerouting an existing road or trail to protect Recreation Area resources from degradation, or to protect public safety, as determined to be appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Nothing in this section prohibits the use of snowmobiles and other over snow vehicles within the Recreation Area.

children.heading: Winter recreation use plan
children.label: B Winter recreation use plan
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Secretary shall undertake a winter recreation use planning process, which shall include opportunities for use by snowmobiles or other over snow vehicles in appropriate areas of the Recreation Area.March 12, 2019

children.heading: Applicable law
children.label: 5 Applicable law
children.text: Activities authorized under this subsection shall be consistent with the applicable forest plan and travel management plan for, and any law (including regulations) applicable to, the Ashley National Forest.

children.heading: Existing access
children.label: 1 Existing access
children.text: The designation of the Recreation Area shall not affect the ability of authorized users to access, operate, and maintain water infrastructure facilities within the Recreation Area in accordance with applicable authorizations and permits.

children.label: i
children.text: access, including motorized access, for repair and maintenance to water infrastructure facilities within the Recreation Area, including Whiterocks Reservoir, subject to such terms and conditions as the Secretary determines to be necessary; and

children.label: ii
children.text: access and maintenance by authorized users and local governmental entities for the continued delivery of water to the Ashley Valley if water flows cease or become diminished due to impairment of the karst system, subject to such terms and conditions as the Secretary determines to be necessary.
applicable law (including regulations);
the purposes of the Recreation Area; and
the guidelines set forth in the report of the Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs of the House of Representatives accompanying H.R. 5487 of the 96th Congress (H. Rept. 96–617).

children.heading: Fish and wildlife
children.label: j Fish and wildlife
children.text: Nothing in this section affects the jurisdiction of the State with respect to the management of fish and wildlife on Federal land in the State.

children.heading: Wildlife water projects
children.label: k Wildlife water projects
children.text: The Secretary, in consultation with the State, may authorize wildlife water projects (including guzzlers) within the Recreation Area.
constitutes an express or implied reservation by the United States of any water rights with respect to the Recreation Area;
affects any water rights in the State;
affects the use or allocation, in existence on , of any water, water right, or interest in water;March 12, 2019
affects any vested absolute or decreed conditional water right in existence on , including any water right held by the United States;March 12, 2019
affects any interstate water compact in existence on ; orMarch 12, 2019
shall be considered to be a relinquishment or reduction of any water rights reserved or appropriated by the United States in the State on or before .March 12, 2019
all forms of entry, appropriation, and disposal under the public land laws;
location, entry, and patent under the mining laws; and
operation of the mineral leasing, mineral materials, and geothermal leasing laws.

children.heading: Vegetation management
children.label: n Vegetation management
children.text: Nothing in this section prevents the Secretary from conducting vegetation management projects, including fuels reduction activities, within the Recreation Area for the purposes of improving water quality and reducing risks from wildfire.

children.heading: Wildland fire operations
children.label: o Wildland fire operations
children.text: Nothing in this section prohibits the Secretary, in consultation with other Federal, State, local, and Tribal agencies, as appropriate, from conducting wildland fire treatment operations or restoration operations in the Recreation Area, consistent with the purposes of this section.

children.heading: Recreation fees
children.label: p Recreation fees
children.text: Except for fees for improved campgrounds, the Secretary is prohibited from collecting recreation entrance or recreation use fees within the Recreation Area.

children.heading: Communication infrastructure
children.label: q Communication infrastructure
children.text: Nothing in this section affects the continued use of, and access to, communication infrastructure (including necessary upgrades) within the Recreation Area, in accordance with applicable authorizations and permits.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Nothing in this section affects non-Federal land or interests in non-Federal land within the Recreation Area.

children.heading: Access
children.label: 2 Access
children.text: The Secretary shall provide reasonable access to non-Federal land or interests in non-Federal land within the Recreation Area.

children.heading: Outfitting and guide activities
children.label: s Outfitting and guide activities
children.text: Outfitting and guide services within the Recreation Area, including commercial outfitting and guide services, are authorized in accordance with this section and other applicable law (including regulations).
